blocks.content: The group settled back around the fire. DeShawn started a story about his first semester that had everyone laughing inside thirty seconds. The field went easy.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: Kit. How long.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: How long have you been carrying that.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: We all knew, man. Every single one of us.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I wasn't that obvious.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: Homecoming sophomore year. You watched her walk in and I was standing right next to you and I could see it all over your face.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I was not that obvious.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were a little obvious.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You knew.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I suspected. There's a difference.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: I told Marcus in ninth grade. Those two are going to end up together, they just don't know it yet.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: He said it. I have witnesses.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I've been saying it since before that.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: So have I.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: How long has everyone known but us.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Long enough that this is not a surprise to anyone sitting at this fire.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We missed a memo apparently.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: You were the memo. You were the whole memo.
Daisy leaned into Kit's shoulder and he put his arm around her without thinking — out loud, in front of everyone,.
